chi: noun. The twenty-second letter of the Classical and Modern Greek alphabets.
chit: noun. A child or babe; a young, small, or insignificant person or animal.
choir: noun. A group of people who sing together; a company of people who are trained to sing together.
cis: adjective. Having two mutations on two genes on the same chromosome of a homologous pair.
cist: noun. A small receptacle for sacred utensils carried in festivals in Ancient Greece.
coir: noun. The fibre obtained from the husk of a coconut, used chiefly in making rope, matting and as a peat substitute.
cos: noun. Synonym of romaine lettuce, a long-leaved variety of lettuce.
cosh: noun. A weapon made of leather-covered metal similar to a blackjack.
cost: verb. To incur a charge of; to require payment of a (specified) price.
cot: noun. A simple bed, especially one for portable or temporary purposes.
hic: interjection. An approximation to the sound of a hiccup, used e.g. to indicate drunkenness.
hist: interjection. An utterance used to discreetly attract someone's attention.
historic: adjective. Very important; noteworthy: having importance or significance in history.
hoist: verb. To raise; to lift; to elevate (especially, to raise or lift to a desired elevation, by means of tackle or pulley, said of a sail, a
horst: noun. A block of the Earth 's crust, bounded by faults, that has been raised relative to the surrounding area.
host: noun. One which receives or entertains a guest, socially, commercially, or officially.
hot: adjective. Relating to heat and conditions which produce it.
hots: noun. A condition of physical attraction toward (for) someone.
ichor: noun. The liquid said to flow in place of blood in the veins of the gods.
iris: noun. A plant of the genus Iris, common in the northern hemisphere, and generally having attractive blooms (See Iris (plant) on Wikipedia.
itch: noun. A sensation felt on an area of the skin that causes a person or animal to want to scratch said area.
orc: noun. Any of several large, ferocious sea creatures, now especially the killer whale.
orchis: noun. An orchid; now specifically, a plant of the genus Orchis.
orchitis: noun. A painful inflammation of one or both testes.
ort: noun. A fragment; a scrap of leftover food; any remainder; a piece of refuse.
ostrich: noun. A large flightless bird of the order Struthioniformes.
otic: adjective. Of, relating/pertaining to, or located near the ear.
rho: noun. The seventeenth letter of the Modern Greek and Classical alphabets and the nineteenth letter of Old and Ancient.
rich: adjective. Wealthy: having a lot of money and possessions.
riot: noun. A tumultuous disturbance of the public peace by a large group of people, often involving violence or damage to property.
rishi: noun. A Vedic poet and seer who composed Rigvedic hymns, who alone or with others invokes the deities with poetry of a sacred character.
roc: noun. An enormous mythical bird in Eastern legend.
rot: verb. To suffer decomposition due to biological action, especially by fungi or bacteria.
roti: noun. A kind of unleavened flatbread commonly consumed in South Asia and the Caribbean.
rots: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of rot
scot: noun. A local tax, paid originally to the lord or ruler and later to a sheriff.
shirt: noun. An article of clothing that is worn on the upper part of the body, and often has sleeves, either long or short, that cover the arms.
shit: noun. Usually solid excretory product evacuated from the bowels; feces.
short: adjective. Having a small distance from one end or edge to another, either horizontally or vertically.
sic: adverb. Thus; as written; used to indicate, for example, that text is being quoted as it is from the source.
sir: noun. A man of a higher rank or position.
sit: verb. To be in a position in which the upper body is upright and supported by the buttocks.
sri: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Siriano.
stich: noun. A verse, of whatever measure or number of feet, especially a verse of Scripture.
stir: verb. To disturb the relative position of the particles (of a liquid or similar) by passing an object through it.
stoic: noun. Proponent of stoicism, a school of thought, from in 300 B.C.E. up to about the time of Marcus Aurelius, who holds that by cultivating
thoric: adjective. relating to, or containing thorium
tic: noun. A sudden, nonrhythmic motor movement or vocalization.
tics: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of tic
tor: noun. A craggy outcrop of rock on the summit of a hill, created by the erosion and weathering of rock.
torch: noun. A stick of wood or plant fibres twisted together, with one end soaked in a flammable substance such as resin or tallow and set on
toric: adjective. Pertaining to or shaped like a torus, or a section of a torus; toroidal.
torii: noun. A traditional Japanese gate at Shinto shrines, symbolically marking the transition from the profane to the sacred.
tosh: noun. Copper; items made of copper.
trio: noun. A group of three people or things.
chorist: noun. A singer in a choir; a chorister.
chort: noun. A seventeenth-century unit of Burgundy glass.
coth: symbol. The hyperbolic function hyperbolic cotangent.
crith: noun. the weight of 1 litre of hydrogen at standard temperature and pressure.
cro: noun. Synonym of bro (“a male comrade or friend”).
crs: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Seychellois Creole.
crt: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Iyojwa'ja Chorote.
cto: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Emberá-Catío.
hoi: interjection. Alternative form of hoy, hey ! a call to gain someone's attention.
hoit: verb. To behave frivolously and thoughtlessly; to play the fool.
ihi: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ihievbe.
ist: noun. A practitioner or supporter of an ism.
och: interjection. general interjection of confirmation, affirmation, and often disapproval.
osi: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Osing.
rhotic: adjective. That allows the phoneme /ɹ/ even when not followed by a vowel, as in bar (/bɑːɹ/) and bard or barred (/bɑːɹd/); who speaks with
rio: noun. Alternative form of ryō (“Japanese ounce”).
roshi: noun. An elderly and revered Buddhist monk.
rost: noun. Alternative form of roust (“strong tide or current”).
sich: noun. An administrative and military centre for the Zaporozhian and Danube Cossacks.
sirih: noun. A vining plant of Sumatra, whose leaves may be chewed with betel nuts or used medicinally to shrink the vagina after childbirth.
sitch: noun. A brook; an occasional small waterway: a ditch, a gutter or drain; a ravine.
sitio: noun. a subdivision of a barangay, typically rural.
tch: interjection. a representation of the dental click used to show disapproval or disgust; tut, tsk
tisic: adjective. Alternative form of phthisic (“consumptive; phthisical”).
tos: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Highland Totonac.
trs: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Chicahuaxtla Triqui.
tsh: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tsuvan.
